title: Oxytropis campestris
intro: Oxytropis campestris, also known as mountain clover, is a perennial legume that grows in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its pink or purple flowers and leafy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxytropis campestris include glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat a range of health conditions, and is also used in traditional remedies for respiratory issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Oxytropis campestris is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is commonly known as the field locoweed or prairie camel's thumb. This plant is native to western North America. Oxytropis campestris is a perennial herb with pink or purple flowers and is often found growing in dry areas and along roadsides. It is classified within the subgenus Oxytropis of the genus Oxytropis.
